other_observers: Terry McEneaney, Romney Bathust, Shirley Bollinger, Craig Caldwell, Fitz Fitzpatrick, Janet Fitzpatrick, Keelin Miller, Marjorie Neal, Alan Neal, Pat Newman, Elaine Price, Tom Seiler, Susan Setterberg, and Karen Wolnik; I understand that this bird has been seen in previous years at this site, but do not know details about them.
specific_location: South end of Lower Latham Reservoir, 40'20'1.54"N, 104'38'2.26"W
description: A hen-plumaged, Mallard-sized Anas duck with darker body plumage than nearby hen Mallards (including the one with which it appeared to be paired), a yellow-olive bill with black nail, and orangy-red legs. In flight, we noticed two white borders to the colored wing speculum (which was blue). See photos for plumage details.Behaviors: Flying and feeding in the company of a female Mallard (which appeared to be of the nominate subspecies), and in the company of other Mallards and Anas ducks. Seen first in flight, later on the water, then while resting in tall grass at border of water, and finally flying away with the female Mallard.Call: none givenPlumage: Definitive alternate
photographer: Photographer: Daniel Lane, Daniel Lane, Daniel Lane, Daniel Lane, Daniel Lane
